req.title: Hr-Admin deletes payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a payroll-run through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
preconditions: authenticated
role:hr-admin
payroll-run module is enabled

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers authenticated hr-admin deleting a payroll-run via DELETE /api/payroll-runs. Key test dimensions: (1) EP – valid authenticated hr-admin with a valid payroll-run ID should get 200/201 and an audit record; (2) NEG – unauthenticated request should get 401, missing/invalid payroll-run ID should get 400, forbidden role should get 403; (3) BVA – deleting a payroll-run that has exactly one record (boundary of existence) transitions state from 'exists' to 'deleted'; (4) STC – the payroll-run lifecycle state transitions from ACTIVE -> DELETED; (5) DT – decision table covering auth x role x valid-id combinations. Tests are self-contained using fixtures for token acquisition and payroll-run seeding, and idempotent via per-test resource creation/teardown.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_valid_payroll_run_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
payroll-run module is enabled
a payroll-run exists with a known ID
actions: Obtain hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token>
Create a payroll-run via <create_payroll_run_fixture> to get a valid run_id
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} with Authorization header
Query audit log via <get_audit_record> for the deleted run_id
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Audit record exists referencing the payroll-run id
trace: R-WEB-039: positive path – authenticated hr-admin deletes a payroll-run
R-WEB-039: expected outcome – response 200/201 with entity id
R-WEB-039: expected outcome – audit record written
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"

def test_hr_admin_deletes_valid_payroll_run_returns_200_and_audit():
    token = <get_hr_admin_token>()
    run_id = <create_payroll_run_fixture>(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert str(run_id) in str(body), "Entity id missing from response body"

    audit = <get_audit_record>(run_id, token)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found for deleted payroll-run"
    assert audit.get("entity_id") == str(run_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_delete_payroll_run_returns_401
preconditions: No Authorization header is provided
payroll-run module is enabled
a payroll-run exists with a known ID
actions: Obtain hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token> to seed a payroll-run
Create a payroll-run via <create_payroll_run_fixture> to get a valid run_id
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} WITHOUT any Authorization header
expected: Payroll-run is NOT deleted (still retrievable by admin)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"

def test_unauthenticated_delete_payroll_run_returns_401():
    admin_token = <get_hr_admin_token>()
    run_id = <create_payroll_run_fixture>(admin_token)

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    verify = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Payroll-run should still exist after failed delete"

    <cleanup_payroll_run_fixture>(run_id, admin_token)

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_delete_payroll_run_returns_403
preconditions: A user with a non-admin role (e.g. 'employee') is authenticated
payroll-run module is enabled
a payroll-run exists with a known ID
actions: Obtain hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token> to seed data
Create a payroll-run via <create_payroll_run_fixture>
Obtain a restricted-role JWT via <get_employee_token>
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} with the employee token
trace: R-WEB-039: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-039: decision table – authenticated but wrong role
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"

def test_forbidden_role_delete_payroll_run_returns_403():
    admin_token = <get_hr_admin_token>()
    run_id = <create_payroll_run_fixture>(admin_token)
    employee_token = <get_employee_token>()

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    verify = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Payroll-run must still exist after forbidden delete"

    <cleanup_payroll_run_fixture>(run_id, admin_token)

tests.name: test_delete_missing_payroll_run_id_returns_400
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
payroll-run module is enabled
actions: Obtain hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token>
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/ with no run_id in the path (empty segment)
expected: HTTP response status is 400 or 404 indicating validation/missing field error
Response body contains an error message
trace: R-WEB-039: error pathway – 400 on validation error (mandatory field: run_id missing)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"

def test_delete_missing_payroll_run_id_returns_400():
    token = <get_hr_admin_token>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code in (400, 404), (
        f"Expected 400 or 404 for missing run_id, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body or "detail" in body, (
        "Response body must contain an error descriptor"
    )

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_last_existing_payroll_run_boundary
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
payroll-run module is enabled
exactly one payroll-run exists (boundary: minimum non-empty collection)
actions: Obtain hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token>
Ensure only one payroll-run exists via <seed_single_payroll_run_fixture>
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} with Authorization header
Attempt to GET /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} to confirm deletion
Query audit log via <get_audit_record>
expected: Subsequent GET returns 404 confirming the record is gone
Audit record exists for the deletion event
trace: R-WEB-039: boundary – deleting the last (single) payroll-run in the system
R-WEB-039: BVA – lower boundary of collection size = 1
R-WEB-039: STC – payroll-run transitions from ACTIVE to DELETED state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"

def test_hr_admin_deletes_last_existing_payroll_run_boundary():
    token = <get_hr_admin_token>()
    run_id = <seed_single_payroll_run_fixture>(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    delete_resp = requests.delete(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {delete_resp.status_code}"
    )

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404, (
        f"Expected 404 after deletion, got {get_resp.status_code}"
    )

    audit = <get_audit_record>(run_id, token)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record missing after boundary deletion"

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_payroll_run_is_idempotent_or_404
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated and token is valid
payroll-run module is enabled
a payroll-run has already been deleted (state: DELETED)
actions: Obtain hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token>
Create and immediately delete a payroll-run to move it to DELETED state
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{run_id} again for the already-deleted run
expected: HTTP response status is 404 or 409 (run no longer exists or conflict on re-delete)
No duplicate audit record is written for the second delete
trace: R-WEB-039: STC – state transition from DELETED state back to DELETED is invalid or idempotent
R-WEB-039: error pathway – 400/404 on attempting to delete non-existent resource
